The deadline (March 7) is the most immediate, and the assessment is a prerequisite for managing the Site A migration cutover. Understanding the impact of the vendor delay is vital for risk mitigation and stakeholder communication.
Budget approval is a high-risk dependency for continued project operations. With a March 10 deadline and one previous rollover, securing funding is critical to avoid work stoppages.

The departure of a subject matter expert (SME) poses a significant risk to the AD migration project. Knowledge transfer must be prioritized before the expert leaves to prevent permanent loss of technical institutional knowledge.
Treasury Board submissions are complex and high-stakes for GC-Cloud initiatives. While the deadline is March 15, the complexity of the task requires early and sustained effort to ensure approval.
Completing the UAT plan is necessary for the testing phase, but it carries a lower risk profile than budget or compliance tasks. It is currently in progress with a March 10 deadline.
While it has the highest rollover count (3), it lacks a firm deadline. It serves as a secondary mitigation strategy for task_001 and should be pursued once immediate critical deadlines are met.
The deadline is relatively far off (April 1) and the risk level is low. This is a standard communication task that does not currently block technical dependencies.
This is an administrative task with no significant risk or impact on project delivery. It has the furthest deadline and should be completed only after all operational tasks are addressed.
